name: read_outcome_tool
summary: Read the result of a background agent launched with `agent_tool` (`background: true`).
Non-blocking by default — pass `timeout_secs` to wait until the agent finishes.
constraints: Identify the agent with the `task_id` returned by `agent_tool` when it spawned the background run.
When the agent was launched with a `contract`, the result is the validated JSON object verbatim; otherwise the result is the agent's plain-text final reply.
A failed run still returns its final text (prefixed with `Failed:` for crashes) — there is no separate error channel. The verdict is implicit in the body.
anti_patterns: To send a message to a still-running peer agent — use `send_message_tool`.
To spawn a new agent — use `agent_tool`.
cautions: IMPORTANT: a result is delivered exactly once on the work inbox when the parent loop next drains it. Calling `read_outcome_tool` does not consume the inbox notification, but it lets the model read the result on demand without waiting for the next drain.
IMPORTANT: with `timeout_secs > 0`, the call blocks the parent's tool-execution slot for that long. Cancel signals are honored, but no other tools run in parallel.
output: - the completed agent's final text (or its validated JSON when a contract was set),
- `Task <id> is still running.` when the call is non-blocking and the agent has not finished,
- `Timed out waiting for task <id> after <n>s.` when `timeout_secs` elapses,
- `Cancelled while waiting for task <id>.` when the run is cancelled,
- `Unknown task id: <id>.` when the id was never recorded.

task_id.description: Identifier returned by `agent_tool` for the background run.

timeout_secs.description: Maximum seconds to wait for the agent to finish. Default `0` returns immediately with the current state.
